per_turn_leaks.student_message: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, not really... to be honest, I was trying to avoid talking about that part because it might sound weird, but sometimes when it's really bad, my arm will feel a bit numb or tingly on one side. But like I said, that's not always there and only happens during the more severe episodes.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I mentioned this already, but when it's really bad, my left arm can feel numb or tingly. It's not always there, but when it is, it's usually on that side. Other than that, it's mostly just that tightness and pressure in my chest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't think so... like I said, it's only really been in my left arm when it's really severe. But to be honest, that's not a big deal, it's just something else that's annoying on top of feeling like someone's sitting on my chest!

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My ankles have been feeling pretty tight lately, to be honest. It's not painful or anything, it's just that they feel a bit swollen and my shoes feel tighter than usual when I put them on in the morning.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, like I said, my ankles have been feeling pretty tight. It's not really painful or anything, it's just that they feel a bit swollen and my shoes feel tighter than usual when I put them on in the morning. But to be honest, I've only really noticed it since these chest symptoms started, so maybe there's some connection?

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, my shoes definitely feel tighter than usual, and my ankles do look a bit puffy when I take my socks off. It's not super noticeable, but it's enough to be a bit annoying when I'm trying to get comfortable after driving for hours.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... sometimes when this happens, I do feel a bit nauseous. It's not like I want to throw up or anything, but there's just this general feeling of discomfort in my stomach that makes me not really hungry and not really wanting to eat. But it's not always there, and it's definitely not the worst part of it - it's still mostly that chest tightness and pressure that's getting to me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My dad had a heart attack when he was 52 and passed away, which is pretty scary considering I'm now 54. And my mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's still going strong at 78. Other than that, I don't think there's any significant history of medical problems in my family.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My dad's heart attack was pretty scary, to be honest. He was only 52 when it happened, and it's been on my mind a lot lately with what I'm going through now. My mom's doing okay, though - she's still managing her hypertension and diabetes, but she seems to be doing alright for someone her age.

(By the way, can we talk more about my medication? I'd really like to get some guidance on how to manage my amlodipine schedule so I don't miss doses. And maybe we could discuss what else might be going on with these symptoms?)
